LAW AND MOTION 301, FEB-06-20206 RE: DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR ORDER DISMISSING THE ACTION OF PLAINTIFFS ROBERT HARRISON, REBECCA HOOTS, HENRY HOWARD, AND ANGEL LIU, AND FOR AN ORDER IMPOSING MONETARY SANCTIONS. THE SUBJECT MATTER IS DISCUSSED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF DISCUSSION, THE SUBJECT MOTION IS CONTINUED TO MAR-06-2026 FOR FURTHER HEARING AS AGREED UPON IN OPEN COURT. COUNSEL IS INSTRUCTED TO FILE BRIEFING FOR THE NEXT HEARING, PURSUANT TO CODE. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

LAW AND MOTION 301, RE: MOTION FOR ORDER DISMISSING THE ACTION OF PLAINTIFFS ROBERT HARRISON, REBECCA HOOTS, HENRY HOWARD, AND ANGEL LIU, AND FOR AN ORDER IMPOSING MONETARY SANCTIONS. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION TO DISMISS PLAINTIFFS REBECCA HOOTS, HENRY HOWARD, AND ANGEL LIU AS A TERMINATING SANCTION IS GRANTED. SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT TO THE COURT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Dismissing The Action Of Plaintiffs Robert Harrison, Rebecca Hoots, Henry Howard, And Angel Liu, And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Mar-30-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS TENANTS AND OWNERS DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION, JOHN STEWART COMPANY, and THE KNOX PARTNERS LIMITED PARTNERSHIP'S Motion For Order Dismissing The Action Of Plaintiffs Robert Harrison, Rebecca Hoots, Henry Howard, And Angel Liu, And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The motion to dismiss plaintiffs Rebecca Hoots, Henry Howard, and Angel Liu as a terminating sanction is granted. Defendants show that these plaintiffs have not complied with the court's order to provide verified discovery. Plaintiffs opposed in a declaration stating they could explain in camera. The court denied the request, explaining that on a discovery motion it cannot consider reasons not provided to the opposing party, and continued the matter for hearing so the plaintiffs could file a supplemental opposition in the public record. Plaintiffs have not provided any further opposition. The court concludes that these plaintiffs have effectively abandoned the case and that dismissal is warranted to avoid prejudice to defendants, who cannot take discovery from absent plaintiffs.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit to the court a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)
